---
title: "Léo Delalandre"
image: docs/documentation/pictures/Delalandre.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  id: hero-heading
  links:
    - icon: mailbox
      text: Mail
      href: mailto:leo.delalandre@protonmail.com
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=s4Le9SQAAAAJ
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/LDelalandre
    - icon: journal-text
      text: Blog
      href: https://leodelalandre.substack.com/ 
---

Trained in ecology and evolution, I worked in plant comparative ecology during my Ph.D. More recently, I have been trying to apply comparative approaches to long-term trends in human social organization, within the cultural evolution framework. Currently at the [Complexity Science Hub](https://csh.ac.at/leo-delalandre/) in Vienna, Austria, I study how gender inequalities have changed during the Holocene, i.e., since the advent of agriculture and the dramatic increase in the size and hierarchy of societies. I use a mix of modelling and data analyses, relying on archaeological and historical data – and most of all on the collaboration with specialists of these disciplines.

You can also visit my blog, [Making our Nature](https://leodelalandre.substack.com/), where I share my work and reflections.
